briefing.opening: Hello everyone. We will now begin the climate briefing for April 24, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Action and Awareness Expansion
sections.content: Today, in Korea, coinciding with 'Climate Change Week,' several local governments including Yeongdo-gu, Hapcheon-gun, Ansan Green Environment Center, Changnyeong-gun, and Cheongdo-gun, encouraged citizen participation by launching the '10 Minutes of Rest for Earth' campaign and carbon neutrality practice activities [1, 13, 14, 23, 24, 26, 28, 84, 112]. Notably, in Yeosu, the 3rd UNFCCC Climate Week Commemoration Ceremony and a high-level ceremony were held, providing a platform for discussions on international climate cooperation. The Republic of Korea Ministry of Climate also decided to extend its greenhouse gas expert training cooperation with the UNFCCC Secretariat for five years, until 2031 [2, 4, 8, 9, 10, 11, 20, 21, 22, 25, 27, 29, 31, 32, 33, 35, 36, 37, 38, 39, 41, 42, 43, 44, 49, 51, 52, 55, 57, 59, 61, 105]. Furthermore, CJ Olive Young is helping consumers adapt to climate change in their daily lives by introducing its 'Survival Beauty' campaign and special exhibitions to respond to climate change [5, 7, 15, 16, 30, 45, 48, 58, 64, 147]. In addition, diverse efforts continue, including the Korea Meteorological Administration's 'Climate Change Story' contest [12] and GIC's 'Action-Oriented' Climate Change Response Strategy Forum [3, 46].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating the Transition to a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are accelerating across various sectors in Korea. The National Police Agency is accelerating carbon neutrality practices in public safety operations through the introduction of solar power generation and electric patrol cars, and has announced plans to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 37.4% by 2030 [65, 66, 67, 86, 90, 93, 95, 99, 101, 106, 107, 108, 109, 115]. Saemangeum is set to leap forward as a future innovative city based on AI and carbon neutrality with the approval of its smart city plan [68, 73, 75, 110], and the Gwangju Climate & Energy Agency and the Korea Carbon Neutral Agriculture Association have signed an MOU to spread carbon-neutral agricultural technologies [69, 76]. Samsung Electronics Semiconductor Division strengthened its carbon-neutral initiatives with lights-out and waste heat recovery activities for 'Earth Day' [71, 114, 120], and Chungnam Province officially launched labor transition policies in response to carbon neutrality and the AI transformation [94, 96]. Various entities, including corporations, public institutions, and local governments, are accelerating the realization of carbon neutrality through resource circulation [77, 91, 102], eco-friendly education [79, 81, 100, 104], and eco-friendly technology research [78, 80, 118].

sections.title: ⚠️ Real Threats and Responses to Climate Change
sections.content: Climate change is already posing a serious threat to our lives and environment, which is clearly evident in domestic and international news. Domestically, climate change is leading to an increase in heatstroke, infectious diseases, and allergic conditions [6]. Ecological changes are also evident, such as the earlier dispersal of pine pollen and the difficulty in finding butterflies in flower gardens [17, 18]. Particularly, on 'World Penguin Day,' an unusual tidal wave covered penguin habitats, forcing changes in nesting locations, indicating severe impacts on wildlife [53, 162]. Internationally, an increasing trend of wildfires due to climate change is observed in Georgia, USA [149, 151, 163, 164], and Idaho is experiencing persistent low snowfall and severe drought [160, 161]. In response to these threats, the Seoul Metropolitan Government is proactively addressing climate change by inspecting the Gwanghwamun Deep Underground Rainwater Tunnel [19], and the Korea Rural Community Corporation is strengthening climate change-responsive water management by holding on-site water management briefings [34, 50]. Additionally, Seongbuk-gu is applying heat-reflective paint to homes of heat-vulnerable populations [146], and experts are predicting a 'Super El Niño' this year, warning that conditions could form to break global heat records [177].

sections.title: 🌍 The Light and Shadow of Renewable Energy Transition and International Cooperation
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is a global challenge, but it faces various challenges and opportunities both domestically and internationally. Domestically, the Korea National Railway has launched research into renewable energy use, presenting an RE100 roadmap to transition 100% of its electric railway power to renewable energy [124, 127, 137]. Korea Western Power has significantly expanded its renewable energy organization and personnel, setting a target to achieve 13.9 GW by 2040 [129, 130]. The Minister of Climate declared an era of renewable energy in response to high oil prices from the Middle East, stating plans to expand renewable energy capacity to 100 GW by 2030 and promptly establish an 'energy highway' [141, 142]. However, despite electricity consumers urging a shift to a renewable energy-centric basic plan for electricity supply and demand [123], criticism has been raised that Korea ranks among the lowest in renewable energy among OECD countries [125]. Furthermore, there are opinions that renewable energy has limitations in meeting the surging power demand of the AI and semiconductor era [143], and difficulties in the transition process also exist, such as 60 global companies, including Amazon, opposing moves to strengthen greenhouse gas emission reporting [139]. Internationally, while there are positive developments such as US GM transitioning 100% of its operations' electricity to renewable energy [133] and GS E&C expanding into the Indian renewable energy market [136], concerns are also being raised that international political factors could negatively impact climate action, such as claims that the US-Israel's Iran war is accelerating climate change [154, 176] and the Florida Governor signing a bill to roll back climate change measures [153].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has clearly demonstrated the diverse domestic and international efforts to respond to climate change, alongside the severe threats we still face and the challenges that need to be addressed. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Seongbuk-gu Office exterior. Provided by Seongbuk-gu. Seoul's Seongbuk-gu announced on the 24th that it will implement a 'heat-reflective paint installation project for vulnerable populations' to ensure the safety of residents susceptible to heatwaves. This initiative aims to prevent heatwave damage, mitigate the urban heat island effect, adapt to the climate crisis, and prevent heat-related illnesses. By applying heat-reflective paint, which reflects sunlight and solar heat, to roofs, the project seeks to reduce heat accumulation in buildings and prevent indoor temperature increases.

summary.phenomenon: Seoul's Seongbuk-gu announced on the 24th that it will proceed with a heat-reflective paint installation project for 37 homes inhabited by heatwave-vulnerable residents.
summary.cause: This project was initiated to prevent heatwave damage, mitigate the urban heat island effect, adapt to the climate crisis, and prevent heat-related illnesses.
summary.outlook: It is expected that the application of heat-reflective paint will reduce heat accumulation in buildings and prevent indoor temperature increases.

articles.original_title: CJ올리브영, ‘서바이벌 뷰티’로 폭염 맞선다
articles.description: Provided by CJ Olive Young. CJ Olive Young will run a 'Survival Beauty' campaign until July. Survival Beauty refers to a beauty trend focused on protecting and managing skin in hot and humid environments. According to CJ Olive Young on the 24th, as summers lengthen due to recent climate change, there's a growing number of consumers seeking comprehensive skin care that goes beyond UV protection to address issues like heat sensation, oiliness, and breakouts.
